ec2.vpnconnection.CfnAbout:
Specifies a VPN connection between a virtual private gateway and a VPN customer gateway or a transit gateway and a VPN customer gateway.

To specify a VPN connection between a transit gateway and customer gateway, use the `TransitGatewayId` and `CustomerGatewayId` properties.

To specify a VPN connection between a virtual private gateway and customer gateway, use the `VpnGatewayId` and `CustomerGatewayId` properties.

For more information, see [AWS Site-to-Site VPN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpn/latest/s2svpn/VPC_VPN.html) in the *AWS Site-to-Site VPN User Guide*.

ec2.vpnconnection.CustomerGatewayId.Description: The ID of the customer gateway at your end of the VPN connection.

ec2.vpnconnection.StaticRoutesOnly.Description: Indicates whether the VPN connection uses static routes only. Static routes must be used for devices that don't support BGP.  If you are creating a VPN connection for a device that does not support Border Gateway Protocol (BGP), you must specify `true`.

ec2.vpnconnection.Tags.Description: Any tags assigned to the VPN connection.

ec2.vpnconnection.TransitGatewayId.Description: The ID of the transit gateway associated with the VPN connection.  You must specify either `TransitGatewayId` or `VpnGatewayId`, but not both.

ec2.vpnconnection.VpnGatewayId.Description: The ID of the virtual private gateway at the AWS side of the VPN connection.  You must specify either `TransitGatewayId` or `VpnGatewayId`, but not both.

ec2.vpnconnection.VpnTunnelOptionsSpecif.Description: The tunnel options for the VPN connection.
